pages.title: Steps to Calories Calculator (Free) – Walking Burn Estimate
pages.description: Estimate calories burned from steps walked using pace and terrain assumptions; optional daily-average totals with weight-change disclaimers.
pages.body:
Steps to Calories Calculator

Estimates calories burned from walking using your step count, body weight, an assumed stride length (refined if you enter height), walking pace, and terrain. Surface, incline, speed changes, fitness level, footwear, carrying weight, and how your watch counts steps can shift real energy use up or down—treat this as a planning band, not a lab measurement. Optional totals assume your step entry is a typical daily average and use rough fat-equivalent math (~3,500 kcal per lb, ~7,700 per kg) only if diet and everything else stayed identical—real scale weight moves with water, salt, hormones, and appetite too.

Result
—

pages.title: Calorie Deficit Planner (Free) – Goal Timeline vs Maintenance
pages.description: Plan average daily calorie deficit or surplus from goal weight, timeline, and maintenance calories—with rule-of-thumb energy density notes.
pages.body:
Calorie Deficit Planner

Turn a weight goal and timeline into an average daily calorie deficit or surplus versus the maintenance calories you enter. Use the daily calorie calculator first if you need a maintenance estimate from age, height, weight, and activity. This tool applies rough energy-density rules (~3,500 kcal per lb, ~7,700 kcal per kg of tissue change) spread evenly across the days you choose—real bodies fluctuate with water, hormones, and adherence.

Result
—

pages.title: Heart Rate Zone Calculator (Free) – Training Bands by Age
pages.description: Estimate heart rate training zones using age-based max HR and optional resting HR for Karvonen zones.
pages.body:
Heart Rate Zone Calculator

Estimates training heart-rate zones using your age (for maximum heart rate) and, if you enter it, resting heart rate for the Karvonen (heart-rate reserve) method. These are fitness-planning bands, not medical targets—medications, fatigue, heat, and fitness level change how hard the same pace feels.

Result
—

pages.title: Navy Body Fat Calculator (Free) – Tape Method Estimate
pages.description: Estimate body fat percentage from neck, waist, and hip circumferences using the U.S. Navy circumference formula.
pages.body:
Navy Body Fat Calculator

Estimates body fat percentage from circumference measurements using the U.S. Navy tape method (log formula). Men need neck and waist (waist measured at the navel); women need neck, waist at the narrowest point, and hips at the widest. Results are service-screening style estimates, not hydrostatic weighing or DEXA—tension on the tape and measurement sites dominate error.

Result
—

pages.title: Waist-to-Height Ratio Calculator (Free) – WHtR Screening Tool
pages.description: Compute waist-to-height ratio from waist and height in the same units and compare to common screening benchmarks.
pages.body:
Waist-to-Height Ratio Calculator

Waist-to-height ratio (WHtR) compares waist circumference to height using the same units. It reflects central adiposity more directly than weight alone. A common public-health screening cue is keeping waist under about half of height (WHtR below 0.5)—cutoffs vary by age, sex, and ethnicity; this page does not diagnose disease.

Result
—

pages.title: Water Intake Calculator (Free) – Daily Fluid Planning Estimate
pages.description: Estimate a daily fluid planning range from weight, activity, exercise minutes, and climate—with medical-disclaimer context.
pages.body:
Water Intake Calculator

Estimates a daily fluid planning range from body weight plus simple bumps for activity and heat. It counts all fluids toward the total unless your clinician gave you a restriction—many people meet part of this through food. Kidney disease, heart failure, pregnancy, breastfeeding, or fluid limits mean you should follow medical guidance instead of online estimates.

Result
—

pages.title: Debt-to-Income Calculator (Free) - Check DTI Ratio
pages.description: Estimate your debt-to-income ratio using total monthly debt payments and gross monthly income.
pages.body:
Debt-to-Income (DTI) Calculator

Back-end DTI here means total minimum monthly debt payments ÷ gross monthly income, expressed as a percent ((debt ÷ income) × 100). Front-end ratios (housing-only vs income) and which debts belong in the numerator differ by lender and loan program. Related pages run separate arithmetic only: Mortgage calculator, Loan calculator, APR calculator.

Definitions (informational)

  • Front-end DTI — Some underwriting contexts compare housing-related payment to gross income; exact definitions vary.
  • Back-end DTI — Total recurring debt payments compared to gross income; included debts vary.
  • Figures in third-party sources — Articles and program materials sometimes reference percentages such as 28%, 36%, or 43%; they are not universal rules.

This page does not assess creditworthiness or eligibility. For requirements that affect you, refer to official loan/program disclosures or a qualified professional.

Arithmetic examples

  • $5,000 gross income, $1,200 debts → DTI ≈ 24%.
  • $6,500 gross income, $2,600 debts → DTI = 40%.

Result
—

pages.title: Loan Fee Annualizer (Free) – Simple Yearly Rate for Comparison
pages.description: Annualizes total upfront loan fees as a simple percent of principal per year. For planning only—not a Truth-in-Lending (TILA) disclosure APR.
pages.body:
Loan fee annualizer (simple)

Spread total upfront fees evenly over the loan term. The result is a simple yearly percentage for rough comparison only—it is not the APR on a federal Truth in Lending disclosure. For note rate + fees → APR (actuarial estimate), use the APR calculator.

Result
—

pages.title: APR Calculator (Free) – Actuarial Estimate from Rate, Fees & Term
pages.description: Estimate APR on a fixed-rate amortizing loan when upfront fees reduce net proceeds: monthly IRR × 12. Not a Truth-in-Lending (TILA) disclosure substitute.
pages.body:
APR calculator (estimate)

Uses your stated note rate (compounded monthly), loan principal, term, and upfront fees that reduce net cash received at closing while payments still amortize the full principal. Solves the monthly rate so the present value of payments equals net proceeds, then shows actuarial APR ≈ 12 × monthly IRR. Ignores PMI, escrow, odd days, prepaid per-diem interest, and Reg Z fee rules—not your Loan Estimate APR. The loan fee annualizer describes fee-only annualization without modeling a separate note rate.

Result
—

Related calculators: Loan calculator · Mortgage calculator · Simple interest calculator · Compound interest · APY calculator · Implied loan rate (payment solver)

What this tool illustrates

  • Fees netted from proceeds — Net cash at closing is below principal while scheduled payments amortize the stated principal at the note rate.
  • Note rate vs displayed APR estimate — The solved rate folds fee timing into an annualized figure alongside the stated note rate.
  • Regulatory APR — Truth-in-Lending / Regulation Z disclosures use definitions, fee sets, and rounding not reproduced here.

APR vs APY

APR here reflects loan cash flows: payments versus net funding, expressed as an annualized rate from the solved monthly IRR.

APY describes how an earning balance grows when interest compounds within the year (for example savings). The APY calculator converts nominal rate and compounding frequency into APY.

Worked examples ($320k principal, 30-year, 6.5% note)

  • $0 upfront fees — Net proceeds equal principal; actuarial APR matches the note rate in this model.
  • $6,500 fees deducted from proceeds — Net cash at closing is lower while payments still amortize $320k; the displayed APR estimate exceeds the note rate in this model.

Equation solved

Let M be the monthly payment amortizing principal P at the stated note rate, and N = P − fees net proceeds. The tool finds monthly IRR r such that

N = Σk=1n M ⁄ (1 + r)k

then reports actuarial APR ≈ 12 × r and an effective annual rate for comparison.

pages.title: APY Calculator (Free) – Nominal Rate to Annual Percentage Yield
pages.description: Convert a nominal annual rate and compounding frequency into APY: (1 + r/n)^n − 1. For deposits and investments, not loan disclosures.
pages.body:
APY Calculator

Converts a nominal annual rate into annual percentage yield (APY) assuming interest compounds at a fixed number of periods per year. Loan APR reflects borrowing cash flows under different definitions; that formulation is on the APR calculator page.

Result
—

pages.description: Solve nominal annual interest (monthly compounding) from loan principal, monthly payment, and term—inverse of standard amortization.
pages.body:
Loan implied rate calculator

Fixed-rate, level-payment model: solves for the nominal annual rate with monthly compounding such that the payment amortizes the principal over the term. Related pages on this site: Loan calculator, Mortgage calculator; combined note rate and upfront fees are modeled on the APR calculator.
